questions.question: Which of the following addresses are identical to each other: 1. Rahul K 45/18 Block-C, Rajendra Nagar, Kanpur, 208005 2. Rahul K. 45/18 Block-C, Rajendra Nagar, Kanpur, 208005 3. Rahul K. 45/18 Block-C, Rajendra Nagar, Kanpur, 208005 4. Rahul K. 45/18 Block C, Rajendra Nagar, Kanpur, 208005
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 2 & 3. The addresses of 2 and 3 are identical.

questions.question: Select the correct combination of mathematical signs that can sequentially replace * signs from left to right to balance the following equation. 72 * 88 * 8 * 23 * 8 * 245

Which of the following letter-clusters should replace # and % so that the pattern and relationship followed between the letter-cluster pair on the left side of :: is the same as that on the right side of :: ? # : AKG :: PTS : %
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. # = CHK, % = NWO. Option 2: # = CHK, % = NWO CHK : AKG → C - 2 = A, H + 3 = K, K - 4 = G PTS : NWO → P - 2 = N, T + 3 = W, S - 4 = O

questions.question: The second number in the given number- pairs is obtained by performing certain mathematical operation(s) on the first number. The same operation(s) is/are followed in all the number-pairs except one. Find that odd number-pair.
Which element is fourth to the left of 71 in step III?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Gold. The words are arranged in alphabetical order. The order is Asics, Eat and Gold. The numbers are arranged in descending order. The order is 93, 71, 63 By following the same logical pattern, we get, Input:- 71 Eat Gold 63 93 Asics Step I:- Asics Eat Gold 63 93 71 Step II:- Eat Asics Gold 93 71 63 Step III:- Gold Eat Asics 93 71 63 Here, the word ‘Gold’ is fourth to the left of 71 in step III.

questions.question: In a code language, ‘MANGO’ is written as ‘PHOBN’. How will ‘LEMON’ be written as the same language?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. OPNFM. ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/RP6.1.png)

questions.question: Select the combination of letters that, when sequentially placed in the blanks of the given series, will completethe series. TN_ET_TN_E_ _T_ KE_O
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. KOKTONT. TNKETO TNKETO TNKETO KOKTONT

questions.question: A number has been denoted to each of the given letters. Select the option from the following four possible arrangements of these numbers that forms a meaningful word. 1 = M, 2 = S, 3 = E, 4 = T, 5 = D, 6 = C, 7 = O, 8 = I
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 57132486. D₍₅₎ O₍₇₎ M₍₁₎ E₍₃₎ S₍₂₎ T₍₄₎ I₍₈₎ C₍₆₎&ₙbₛₚ; = 57132486 = DOMESTIC

questions.question: If ‘A # B’ means ‘A is the sister of B’ and ‘A @ B’ means ‘A is the mother of B’, then which of the following expressions means ‘D is the mother of G’?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. D @ I # N # G. ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Rp9.1.png) Option 4 indicates that D is the mother of G.

questions.question: Read the given statements and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements. Statements: All Mangoes are fruits. Some fruits are Vegetables. All vegetables are Bananas. Conclusions: (I): All Mangoes are Banana. (II): Some Fruits are Bananas.
options: Either Conclusions I or II follow
Neither Conclusion I nor II follows
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Only Conclusion II follows. ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Rp10.1.png) Only conclusion II follows.

questions.question: Find the missing term in the following series: 8, 24, 28, 140, 146, 1022, ?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 1030. ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Rp11.1.png)

questions.question: In a certain code language, ‘GOLD’ is written as ‘190’, and ‘SOLD’ is written as ‘250’. How will ‘FISH’ be written in the same code language?
Which of the following would be at the third position in the English dictionary? I. Examination II. Exaggerate III. Exactness IV. Exacerbate V. Example
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Exaggerate. IV, III, II, I, V (IV) Exacerbate, (III) Exactness (II) Exaggerate (I) Examination (V) Example

questions.question: By interchanging the given two numbers which of the following equations will be correct? 8 and 12 I. 8 × 9 – 96 ÷ 12 + 48 = 144 II. 192 ÷ 8 + 74 – 12 × 10 = 10
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Both I and II. I. (12 × 9) – (96 ÷ 8) + 48 = 144 = 108 – 12 + 48 = 144 (True) II. (192 ÷ 8) + 74 – (12 × 10) = 10 = 16 + 74 – 80 = 10 (True) Both are correct.

questions.question: 12 is related to 149 following a certain logic. Following the same logic, 17 is related to 294. To which of the following is 18 related, following the same logic? [NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits. E.g. 13 – Operations on 13 such as adding/subtracting /multiplying etc. to 13can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1and 3 is not allowed]
Based on the numerical analogy given, choose the correct answer from the options given below? 9 : 324 :: 6 : 144 :: 7 : ?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 196. Pattern = a : \((a\times2)^2\) i.e. \(9:324\rightarrow(9\times2)^2={18}^2\rightarrow324\) and \(6:144\rightarrow(6\times2)^2\rightarrow144\) Similarly \(7:196\rightarrow\left(7\times2\right)^2\rightarrow196 \)

questions.question: Find the option that is related to the fifth term in the same way as the second term is related to the first term and fourth is related to third term. GIRL : ILVQ :: FEAR : HHEW : LAND : ?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. NDRI. ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Rp17.1.png) ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Rp17.2.png)

questions.question: Identify the number which does not satisfy the following condition: Sum of the digits is a divisor of the number.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 105. 2+1+6 = 9, which is a divisor of 216 4+0+8 = 12, which is a divisor of 408 2+0+0 = 2, which is a divisor of 200 1+0+5 = 6, which is not a divisor of 105.

questions.question: You are traveling in a train and notice a 6-year-old child looking lost and anxious, sitting alone without any adult accompanying him. What should be your first course of action?
options: Wait and see if any adult comes to claim the child.
Gently approach the child and ask about his family or guardian and if he knows their location or inform the railway police.
Call the child helpline number directly.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Gently approach the child and ask about his family or guardian and if he knows their location or inform the railway police. (). The safest and most constructive first step is to calmly approach the child and talk to him in a friendly manner, asking if he knows where his parents or guardian are and if he can provide any details about them. Since the child is 6 years old, he might be able to communicate basic information, such as names, seat numbers, or other helpful details. if not, you can then escalate by informing railway police.

questions.question: Convert the following binary numbers to decimal numbers. 10111₂
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 23. 1 0 1 1 1 \(1\times2^4 + 0\times2^3 + 1\times2^2 + 1\times2^1 + 1\times2^0\) 16 + 0 + 4 + 2 + 1 = 23

questions.question: Identify the diagram that best represents the relationship among the given classes: Male, Teacher, Knowledgeable
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Rp22.1.png) . ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Rp22.5.png) Hence, 1 is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the set in which the numbers are related in the same way as are the numbers of the following set. (NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits.). (4 15 1) (7 22 3)
Similarly, identify the set for the word “SCOPE”. ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/16.png)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 20, 34, 11, 03, 12. The given code follows the logic as follows: First digit of the number is taken from the row and subsequently, the second digit is taken from the column. Thus, the correct code for SCOPE is 20, 34, 11, 03, 12.

questions.question: A, B, C, D and E live in a five-floor building where the ground floor is numbered Floor 1, and topmost floor is numbered Floor 5. No two people live on the same floor. There are two floors between E and C, where E lives above C. B lives immediately below E. There is one floor between A and B. D lives immediately below B. Then who among the following lives on Floor 3?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. B. Person Floor 5 A Floor 4 E Floor 3 B Floor 2 D Floor 1 C Hence, B lives on the floor 3.

questions.question: Who inaugurated the Saras Aajeevika Food Festival 2025 in New Delhi?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Shivraj Singh Chouhan. Union Minister for Rural Development, Agriculture and Farmers’ Welfare, Shri Shivraj Singh Chouhan, inaugurated the Saras Aajeevika Food Festival 2025 at Sunder Nursery in New Delhi on December 1, 2025. Organized by the Ministry of Rural Development, the event features over 500 delicacies from 25 states prepared by SHG women to promote rural entrepreneurship and self-reliance.

questions.question: The total number of images formed by two mirrors inclined at 120° to each other is _______ .
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. 2. Number of Images formed= (360°/θ) -1 (360° /120° ) − 1 = 2

questions.question: In which Harappan site was a paved bathroom found?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Mohenjodaro. Mohenjodaro is situated on the banks of Indus River in present-day Pakistan. Significant discoveries from Mohenjodaro include the bronze statue of a dancing girl, the sculpture of a bearded priest, and the Great Granary.

questions.question: The financial year in India starts from 1st April and ends on _________.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. 31st March. A financial year, also known as a fiscal year, is a 12-month period used by governments, businesses, and organizations for accounting and financial reporting purposes.

questions.question: Which Articles of the Constitution of India provide for a parliamentary form of government at the Centre?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. Articles 74 and 75. Article 74 – Council of Ministers to aid and advise the President (1) There shall be a Council of Ministers headed by the Prime Minister to assist and advise the President, and the President shall exercise his functions in accordance with such advice. Article 75 – Other provisions relating to Ministers (1) The Prime Minister shall be appointed by the President, and the other Ministers shall be appointed by the President on the advice of the Prime Minister.

questions.question: In the higher latitudes (50°- 70°) of Northern hemisphere the spectacular Coniferous forests are found. These are also called as ______ .
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. Taiga. Taiga, biome (major life zone) of vegetation composed primarily of cone-bearing needle-leaved or scale-leaved evergreen trees, found in northern circumpolar forested regions characterized by long winters and moderate to high annual precipitation . The taiga, “land of the little sticks” in Russian, takes its name from the collective term for the northern forests of Russia, especially Siberia. Taiga- Trees found are mainly cone-bearing trees like spruce, pine and hemlock.

questions.question: Match the following: Temple State A) Mahakaleshwar Jyotirlinga i) Assam B) Somnath Jyotirlinga Temple ii) Odisha C) Kamakhya Temple iii) Madhya Pradesh D) Jagannath Temple iv) Gujarat
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Opttion 3 i.e. (A)-iii (B)-iv (C)-i (D)-ii. Mahakaleshwar Temple – One of the twelve Jyotirlingas, located in Ujjain, Madhya Pradesh, dedicated to Lord Shiva in his fierce Mahakal form. Somnath Temple – A revered Jyotirlinga in Gujarat, known as the “Eternal Shrine,” symbolizing resilience through multiple reconstructions. Kamakhya Temple – A powerful Shakti Peeth in Assam, dedicated to Goddess Kamakhya, renowned for its unique celebration of the Ambubachi Mela. Jagannath Temple – A sacred Vaishnavite shrine in Puri, Odisha, famous for the annual Rath Yatra of Lord Jagannath, Balabhadra, and Subhadra.

questions.question: The reaction of an acid and a metal gives _________ .
options: carbon dioxide, salt and hydrogen gas
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. hydrogen gas and salt. A general reaction of acid with metal can be given as: Metal + Acid → Salt Solution + Hydrogen Gas. The reactions of metals with acids are actually redox reaction. That is, the metal gets oxidised and the acid gets reduced during the reaction.

questions.question: Who was the first person to discuss about the Poverty Line in India?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Dadabhai Naoroji. Dadabhai Naoroji in his book "Poverty and Un-British Rule in India" made earliest estimation of poverty line.

questions.question: Examine the following statements regarding the Civil Disobedience Movement and assess their correctness: • Unlike the Non-Cooperation Movement, the Civil Disobedience Movement involved defying specific colonial laws • The movement was launched after Gandhi’s eleven demands were rejected by Lord Irwin. • The movement remained confined to salt law violation and did not extend to other areas. • It saw wider participation from peasants, women, and business classes than earlier movements. Which of the above statements are correct?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. 1, 2, and 4 only. Statement 1: Correct — CDM involved active law-breaking (salt, forest, tax, etc.). Statement 2: Correct — Gandhi presented 11-point demands; rejection led to Dandi March. Statement 3: Incorrect — It expanded beyond salt to include no tax, forest satyagrahas, etc. Statement 4: Correct — It saw broader class participation, especially women and rural peasants.

questions.question: Sarhul, a famous festival of dance and nature, is celebrated by which tribe in India?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. Oraon. Sarhul- The festival of the Oraon tribe. It is the festival of the New Year and celebrated in the Hindu month of Chaitra, three days after the appearance of the new moon. It is also a celebration of the beginning of spring.

questions.question: Which river flows into the Wular lake in the north and then into Baramulla?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. Jhelum. Jhelum River originates from a spring at Verinag in the southeastern part of the Kashmir Valley. It first flows northward into Wular Lake, located in the northwestern part of the valley. After leaving Wular Lake, it changes direction, flowing southwards.

questions.question: Consider the following statement regarding the National Human Rights Commission (NHRC) of India: It is a statutory body established under the Protection of Human Rights Act, 1993, and is empowered to investigate human rights violations suo motu. Which of the following options is correct with respect to the above statement?
options: The statement is incorrect as NHRC is a constitutional body.
The statement is incorrect, and NHRC can only act on complaints received.
The statement is correct, and NHRC has suo moto powers.
The statement is incorrect as NHRC was established under the Constitution.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. The statement is correct, and NHRC has suo moto powers. The statement is correct, and NHRC has suo moto powers. The National Human Rights Commission (NHRC) is a statutory body established under the Protection of Human Rights Act, 1993. It has the authority to investigate human rights violations suo moto (on its own initiative) or based on complaints.

questions.question: Name the French jeweler who traveled to India at least six times during the Mughal period?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Jean-Baptiste Tavernier. Jean-Baptiste Tavernier came to India during the reign of Shah Jahan. Shah Jahan's regin is described described by two French traveller Bernier and Tavernier and the Italian traveller Nicoli Manucci.

questions.question: Who was sworn in as the President of Ghana on 7 January 2025 after winning the December presidential election?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. John Dramani Mahama. John Mahama sworn in as Ghana’s President on January 7, 2025, marking his return for a final four-year term amidst economic challenges

questions.question: Which method of calculating the national income is also known as ‘Industrial Origin Method’?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. Value-Added Method. The Product Method, also called the Value Added Method, estimates national income by calculating the value added by different industrial sectors (primary, secondary, and tertiary). Since it measures income based on the origin of production from industries, it is also known as the Industrial Origin Method.

questions.question: Which of the following is the most literate district in India as per Census 2011?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. Serchhip. According to the Census of India 2011, Serchhip district of Mizoram recorded the highest literacy rate in the country, at approximately 97.9%. As per Census 2011 : • State with highest literacy rate - Kerala • State with lowest literacy rate - Bihar

questions.question: Which of the following states secured the first position in the Popular Choice category for tableaux (States/UTs) at the Republic Day Parade 2026?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Gujarat. The results of the ‘Popular Choice’ category based on the online poll conducted on the MyGov portal for the citizens to vote for their favourite Tableau and Marching Contingents are as follows: Best Marching Contingent among three Services - Assam Regiment Best Marching Contingent among CAPFs/other auxiliary Forces – CRPF Top three tableau (States/UTs): • Gujarat (Mantra of Swadeshi – Self-Reliance – Freedom: Vande Mataram) • Uttar Pradesh (Culture of Bundelkhand) • Rajasthan (Golden Touch of the Desert: Bikaner Gold Art (Usta Art)) Best tableau from Central Ministries/Departments – Deptt. of School Education & Literacy (National Education Policy 2020: Rocketing Indian School Education on the Path to Viksit Bharat).

questions.question: Identify the correct statement.
options: Resistance of a wire depends on the length and cross-section of the wire.
Specific resistance is the same for all conductors.
Resistance of a wire depends on the length and density of the wire
Specific resistance is dependent on the cross-sectional area of the wire.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. Resistance of a wire depends on the length and cross-section of the wire. The resistance of a wire is directly proportional to its length and inversely proportional to its cross-sectional area.

questions.question: The emergency provisions of the Indian Constitution are borrowed from the Government of India Act, ____________.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. 1935. The Indian Constitution also borrowed various features from the Government of India Act of 1935, including the federal scheme, the office of the governor, the judiciary, and the public service commissions.

questions.question: Which of the following lakes in India is an oxbow lake formed by the meandering of a river is a critical habitat for the endangered Gangetic Dolphin?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. Deepor Beel. Deepor Beel, located in Assam, is an oxbow lake formed by the meandering of the Brahmaputra River. It is a Ramsar site and a critical habitat for the endangered Gangetic Dolphin, along with numerous migratory birds.

questions.question: Which of the following statement(s) is/are correct about Keshav Chandra Sen? A. Keshav Chandra Sen formed the Indian Reform Association in 1870. B. He started a journal for women named Bamabodhini Patrika.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. Both A and B. Keshab Chandra Sen was a prominent 19th century social and religious reformer of Bengal, closely associated with the Brahmo Samaj movement. Sen formed the Indian reform Association in 1870, which persuaded the British Government to enact the Native Marriage Act 1872 (popularly known as Civil Marriage Act).

questions.question: The unit of magnetic intensity in the CGS (centimetre-gram-second) system is _______ .
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. Oersted. Unit of magnetic intensity in the CGS (centimetre- gram- second) system is oersted. In the CGS (Centimeter-Gram-Second) system, magnetic intensity-also called magnetic intensity- also called magnetic field strength – is a vector quality that represents the ability of a magnetic field to magnetize a material. It is denoted by H.

questions.question: Methyl ethyl ketone is also known as:-
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. 2-butanone. Butanone also known as methyl ethyl ketone or ethyl methyl ketone, is an organic compound with formula CH₃CCH₂CH₃. It is a colourless and sweet odor ketone.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a mutation-based disease?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. Malaria. Malaria is not a mutation-based disease; it is caused by the Plasmodium parasite, which is transmitted through the bites of infected Anopheles mosquitoes. On the other hand, Down syndrome is a genetic disorder caused by the presence of an extra chromosome 21. Sickle cell anaemia is a hereditary blood disorder caused by a mutation in the gene that tells the body to make hemoglobin. Phenylketonuria is a genetic disorder resulting from a mutation in the gene for the enzyme phenylalanine hydroxylase, leading to a buildup of phenylalanine in the body.

questions.question: In ∆ADC, the bisector of the internal ∠D and external ∠C meets at B. If ∠DBC = 15° then ∠A is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 30°. ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/mp1.1.png) ∠DAC = 2∠DBC = 2 × 15 = 30°

questions.question: The value of a machine is Rs. 19,200. It decreases by 10% during first year, 12.5% during the second year and 15% during the third year. What will be the value of the machine after 3 years?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Rs. 12,852. Value of machine after 3 years = \(19200 \times\frac{9}{10}\times\frac{7}{8}\times\frac{17}{20}\) = ₹12,852

questions.question: The statistical terms Mean, Median and Mode are known as:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Measures of Central Tendency. Mean, Median and Mode are fundamental measures used to find the central point in a data set. The mean calculates the average. The median identifies the middle value, when the data is ordered (arranged in ascending order). The mode highlights the most frequently occurring value. Together, they describe the central tendency of the data, helping to summarize large amounts of information into meaningful insights about what is typical or expected.

questions.question: Find the value of \(\frac{\sqrt{15.21}}{\sqrt{0.1521}}+\frac{\sqrt{0.0256}}{\sqrt{0.4096}}+\frac{\sqrt{0.36}}{\sqrt{2.25}}\).

A circular decorative wall clock has a radius of 21 cm. A chord joins two points on the circumference such that the chord and the two radii form an equilateral triangle at the center of the clock. The smaller segment formed by this chord is painted in a different color. What percentage of the total area of the clock is painted? (Use π = 3.14 and √3 = 1.732)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 2.88%. Chord and two radii form an equilateral triangle → 60° Radius = 21 cm Area of smaller segment = area of sector – area of triangle \(60°\over360°\) × 3.14 × 441 – \(\frac{\sqrt3}{4}\) × 441 = 441\((\frac{1.57}{3}-\frac{1.732}{4}) \) Area of circle = 3.14 × 441 Required ratio = \(\frac{441(\frac{1.57}{3}-\frac{1.732}{4})}{3.14\times441\ }\times100 \)= \(\frac{\frac{6.28-5.196}{12}}{3.14\ }\times100 \)= \(\frac{108.4}{37.68}\) = 2.88%

questions.question: Atul is able to complete a task in 25 days, while Vipul takes 20 days to complete the same task. If they collaborate and work together for 8 days, what fraction of the work will still remain?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. \(\frac{7}{25}\). Let total work = 100 unit Efficiency ratio of Atul and Vipul = \(\frac{100}{25} : \frac{100}{20}\) = 4:5 Work done by both together in 8 days = (4 + 5) ×8 = 72 unit Fraction of remaining work =\( \frac{28}{100} = \frac{7}{25} \)

questions.question: Sagar started a business by investing Rs. 48000. After 4 months, Saurabh joined with Rs. 72000. After 8 months from the start, Shankar joined with Rs. 96000. If the total profit is Rs. 2,08,000 at the end of the year, find the share of Sagar.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Rs. 78,000. Capital × Time = (48000 × 12) : (72000 × 8) : (96000 × 4) = 3 : 3 :2 Share of Sagar = \(\frac{2,08,000}{(3+3+2)}\) × 3 = Rs. 78,000

questions.question: If sec \((90°-{3θ\over2})\) = \(\sqrt2\), where 0°
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 3. sec \(\bigg(90°-{3θ\over2} \bigg)\)=\(\sqrt2\) sec\(\bigg(90°-{3θ\over2} \bigg)=\sec45^\circ\) \(\bigg(90°-{3θ\over2} \bigg)=45^\circ\) θ = \({45°×2\over3}=30°\) \(2sin\theta+4\ cos2\theta=2\times\frac{1}{2}+4\times\frac{1}{2}\) = 1 + 2 = 3

questions.question: A person lends 25% of his sum of money at 10% per annum, 50% of the rest at 40% per annum and remaining at 20% per annum rate of interest. Find the annual rate of interest, if the interest is calculated on the whole sum?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 25% Annual rate of interest on whole sum = \(\frac{25\times10+37.5\times40+37.5\times20}{100}\) = 25%

questions.question: A pyramid has base of square of length 12 cm. The slant height is 10.4 cm. Find the lateral surface area of the pyramid.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 249.6 cm². Lateral surface area of the pyramid = \(\frac{1}{2}\times\) perimeter of base \times lateral height = \( \frac{1}{2}\times4\times12\times10.4 = 249.6\ cm^2.\)

questions.question: Three friends P, Q, and R are running on a circular track of circumference 1.8 km. They all start from the same point at 7:00 a.m. and run in the same direction with speeds 9 km/h, 12 km/h, and 18 km/h, respectively. How many times will all three meet at the starting point together if they continue running till 11:00 a.m., (excluding the starting point)?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 6 times. Circumference of track = 1.8 km Time taken by them per round: P = 1.8/9 = 12 min, Q = 1.8/12 = 9 min and R = 1.8/18 = 6 min They meet together at the starting point after LCM of (12, 9, 6) minutes = 36 minutes Total time from 7:00 a.m. to 11:00 a.m. = 4 hours = 240 minutes Number of meetings (excluding start) = 240/36 = 6.66 ⇒ 6 times

questions.question: The measures of the three angles of a triangle are in the ratio 9:5:22. Which type of this triangle is?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Obtuse Angled Triangle. Let, all three angles are 9x, 5x and 22x. 9x + 5x + 22x = 180° ⇒ x = 5° Thus, all angles are 45°, 25° & 110° and 110° > 90°, i.e. given measurements are of an obtuse angled triangle.

questions.question: A customer bought 6 kg of premium tea and some quantity of ordinary tea. The price of premium tea per kg was four times the price of ordinary tea. Due to a billing mistake, the quantities of premium and ordinary tea were interchanged, which caused the total bill to increase by 50%. Find the ratio of the original quantity of premium tea to the original quantity of ordinary tea.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 1 : 2. Let price of ordinary tea = ₹x/kg ⇒ price of premium tea = ₹4x/kg Let ordinary tea = y kg Original bill: 6(4x) + y(x) = 24x + yx After interchange: y(4x) + 6(x) = 4yx + 6x Increase = 50% 4y + 6 = 1.5(24 + y) ⇒ y = 12 Required ratio {Premium : Ordinary} = 6 : 12 = 1:2

questions.question: If x – y = 13 and xy = 19, then the value of x²&ⁿᵇˢᵖ;- y² is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 91√5. \(x^2-y^2=\left(x+y\right)\left(x-y\right)\) \(\left(x+y\right)=\sqrt{\left(x-y\right)^2+4xy} \) \(\left(x^2-y^2\right)=7\sqrt5\times13=91\sqrt5\)

questions.question: A bag contains 7 red and 4 green balls. Three balls are drawn at random, find the probability that none of them is red.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. \(\frac{4}{165}\). Probability that none of them is red = \(\frac{4_{C_3}}{{11}_{C_3}}=\frac{4\times3!}{11\times10\times9}=\frac{24}{9\times10\times11}=\frac{4}{5\times11\times3} = \frac{4}{165}\)

questions.question: A hemispherical bowl of radius 24cm is full of liquid. This liquid is to be filled in cylindrical bottles of radius 6cm and height 8cm. Find the number of such containers?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 32. \(\frac{2}{3}\pi (24)^3 = n \times \pi (6)^2 \times 8 \) ⇒ \(n = \frac{2}{3} \times \frac{24 \times 24 \times 24}{36 \times 8} \) ⇒ \(n = 32 \)

questions.question: Two individuals P and Q take a pasture on rent. P grazes 16 horses for 5 months. Q grazes 30 cows for 4 months and 24 sheep for 6 months. However, the following information has been given: • 2 horses are equivalent to 4 cows, • 3 cows are equivalent to 6 sheep. Find what fraction of the total rent should P pay?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 5/11. Given that: 2 horses = 4 cows ⇒ 1 horse = 2 cows 3 cows = 6 sheep ⇒ 1 cow = 2 sheep ⇒ 1 horse = 4 sheep P’s usage = 16 horses for 5 months = 16 × 5 = 80 horse-months Q’s usage = 30 cows for 4 months = 30 cows = 15 horses = 15 × 4 = 60 24 sheep for 6 months 24 sheep = 6 horses = 6 × 6 = 36 Total for Q: 60 + 36 = 96 horse-months Rent ratio = P : Q = 80 : 96 = 5 : 6 P should pay 5/11 of the total rent.

questions.question: The shadow of a vertical tower is √3 times of its height. Find the angle of elevation of the sun.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 30°. ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/sfas.png) tan\(\theta\)=\(\frac{1}{\sqrt3}\)=tan30° ∴ θ = 30°

questions.question: D and E are two points of a triangle on the side AB and AC in a triangle ABC such that DE∥BC. If AD=(x+1), AE=(x−1), DB=(2x+8) and EC=(2x+2), BC=20.8, find DE?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 5.2. ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/adadad.png) \(\frac{x+1}{2x+8}=\frac{x-1}{2\left(x+1\right)}\) = \(\frac{x+1}{2\left(x+4\right)}=\frac{x-1}{2\left(x+1\right)}\) = \(x^2+2x+1=x^2+3x-4\) = x = 5 \(\frac{AD}{BD}=\frac{6}{18}=\frac{1}{3}\) \(\frac{AD}{AB}=\frac{DE}{BC}=\frac{1}{4}\) \(DE=\frac{BC}{4}=\frac{20.8}{4}=5.2\)

questions.question: A trader has a faulty balance that weighs 20% less than what it should weigh. Further he marks up the price of the goods by 20% and he offers 10% discount on marked price. What is his profit percentage?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 35%. Margin factor =\(\frac{100}{80}\times\left(1+\frac{20}{100}\right)\times\frac{9}{10} = \frac{12\times9}{8\times10}=1.35\) Profit = 35%

questions.question: Find the area of the triangle formed by straight lines 2x+3y=6, x+2y=4 with the x-axis? (in sq. unit)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 1. \(2x+3y=6\ = \frac{x}{3}+\frac{y}{2}=1 \) \(x+2y=4 = \frac{x}{4}+\frac{y}{2}=1\) ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/mp21.1.png) Area of the triangle ABC=\(\frac{1}{2}\times\left(4-3\right)\times2\) = 1 sq. unit

questions.question: If a:b = 2:3, b:c = 5:7 and c:d = 8:9 then, find the value of d:c:b:a.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 189 : 168 : 120 : 80. ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/mp23.1.png) a : b : c : d = 80 : 120 : 168 : 189 d : c : b : a = 189 : 168 : 120 : 80

questions.question: If the six-digit number 3x5y4z is exactly divisible by 7, 11 and 13, then {(y+z)÷ x} is equal to:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 2. As we know, 7×11×13 = 1001 Also, abc × 1001 = abcabc Thus, 345 × 1001 = 345345 So, x = 4, y = 3 & z = 5 Hence, (y + z)÷ x = \(\frac{(3+5)}{4}\) = 2

questions.question: The average height of 5 boys is 175 cm. A sixth boy joined the group and the average height of all the boys in the group now increased by one centimetre. The height of the sixth boy is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 181 cm. Height of the sixth boy = 175+(6×1)=181 cm

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words. A person who deliberately avoids giving a clear or direct answer
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Evasive 💢 Explanation: The phrase refers to someone who intentionally avoids clarity and gives indirect or misleading responses, especially to escape commitment or responsibility. The word “evasive” precisely captures this idea, as it denotes deliberate avoidance rather than inability to speak clearly. 👉 Let’s see the meaning of all the words: • Evasive – टालमटोल करने वाला – Deliberately avoiding a direct answer or clear statement. Example: The politician gave evasive replies during the press conference. • Frank – स्पष्ट / ईमानदार – Open, honest, and direct in speech or expression. Example: She was frank about her mistakes. • Articulate – स्पष्ट रूप से बोलने वाला – Able to express ideas clearly and effectively. Example: He is articulate and confident while speaking. • Talkative – बातूनी – Fond of talking a lot, but not necessarily avoiding clarity. Example: The child is very talkative in class.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. To split hairs
options: To waste time on trivial details
To resolve a complex issue carefully
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. To waste time on trivial details 💢 Explanation: The idiom “to split hairs” means to argue over very small, insignificant differences or to focus excessively on minor details that do not affect the main issue. It often carries a negative sense, implying unnecessary or pedantic discussion. 👉 Origin of the Idiom: The idiom comes from the idea that a hair is already extremely thin, so trying to split it further suggests an excessive and unreasonable focus on trivial differences. Example - The lawyer is just trying to split hairs to confuse the jury. Hindi - वह वकील सिर्फ जूरी को भ्रमित करने के लिए बेकार की बारीकी निकाल रहा है।

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.
options: Once she finishes her probation period
she will have been joining the senior
management team to oversee the new project.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. she will have been joining the senior 👉 Replace ‘will have been joining’ with ‘will join’. 📌CONCEPT - For definite future actions occurring after a time clause (once/ after/ when), we use Simple Future, not Future Perfect Continuous. 💢 Detailed Explanation – Joining the senior management team is a one-time future action, not an activity showing duration up to a future point. The use of Future Perfect Continuous is therefore unjustified. Replacing it with “will join” makes the sentence grammatically correct and logically precise.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. Due to financial constraints, the company reported the _____ profit margin among all its competitors this year.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. lowest 📌 Concept/Rule – When comparison is made with all members of a group, the Superlative Degree must be used along with “the.” 💢 Explanation – The phrase “among all its competitors” clearly indicates comparison with more than two entities. Hence, the adjective must be in its superlative form. “The lowest profit margin” correctly completes the sentence, while the other options fail to express total comparison.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. No sooner did the train arrived at the platform than the passengers rushed to board it.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. did the train arrive 📌 CONCEPT – After No sooner + did, the verb must be in the base form due to the auxiliary verb DID. 👉 General Structure: No sooner + did + subject + base verb … than + Simple Past 💢 Explanation – The use of did already marks the tense, so the main verb must remain in its base form. Using arrived after did is incorrect. Therefore, “did the train arrive” correctly completes the sentence and maintains the required inversion pattern.

questions.question: Select the Grammatically correct sentence -
options: Each of the candidates who has applied for the clerical post were asked to appear for the preliminary examination at short notice.
The committee, along with its senior members, have finalized the revised promotion policy after several rounds of discussion.
Hardly had the bank announced the new interest rates than customers started enquiring about long-term deposits.
The manager informed the staff that the new compliance guidelines would be implemented from the beginning of the next financial quarter.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. The manager informed the staff that the new compliance guidelines would be implemented from the beginning of the next financial quarter. 💢 Option (A) - This sentence violates subject–verb agreement in more than one place. • The real subject is “Each”, which is singular and therefore requires a singular verb throughout. • While “has applied” is correct for “each,” the later verb “were asked” is plural and contradicts the singular subject. • The correct form would be “was asked.” Hence, the sentence is grammatically incorrect. 💢 Option (B) - This sentence violates the rule of collective noun agreement. • “Committee” is a collective noun and is treated as singular when acting as a single unit. • The phrase “along with its senior members” is only an additional phrase and does not affect the verb. • Therefore, “have finalized” is incorrect; it should be “has finalized.” Hence, the sentence is grammatically incorrect. 💢 Option (C) - This sentence violates the rule of correlative conjunctions with negative adverbs. • When a sentence begins with “Hardly had…”, it must be followed by “when,” not “than.” • This structure is fixed and frequently tested in bank exams. • Since “than” is incorrectly used, the sentence becomes grammatically incorrect. 💢 Option (D) - This sentence follows all grammatical rules correctly. • The reporting verb “informed” is in the past tense, so the future action correctly changes to “would be implemented.” • Tense sequence, subject–verb agreement, and sentence structure are all correct. • Hence, this is the grammatically correct sentence.

questions.question: Identify the word that is correctly spelt.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Acquaintance. 👉 Acquaintance – परिचय – A person one knows slightly, but who is not a close friend. Example: “He is a casual acquaintance from my previous workplace.”

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. There were too many furniture in the waiting hall for additional seating.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. was too much furniture 📌 Concept/Rule – • “Furniture” is an uncountable noun. • Use much / little (not many / few) with uncountable nouns. • Uncountable nouns take a singular verb. 👉 General Structure: • too / so + much + uncountable noun • Uncountable noun → singular verb (was) 💢 Detailed Explanation – “Furniture” cannot be counted individually, so “many” is incorrect. The correct quantifier is “much,” and since the noun is uncountable, the verb must be singular. Therefore, “was too much furniture” correctly follows both grammatical agreement and quantifier rules.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the following sentence - The government imposed stringent regulations on private coaching institutes to ensure transparency, accountability, and fair practices in fee structures and student admissions.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Strict 💢 Explanation: The word “stringent” refers to rules or regulations that are very strict and rigidly enforced. In the given sentence, the government has imposed firm regulations to control unfair practices. The most appropriate synonym for “stringent” in this context is “strict.” 👉 Let’s see the meanings of all the words: • Stringent – सख़्त / कठोर – Very strict, severe, and firmly enforced. • Example: The factory follows stringent safety rules to prevent accidents. • Strict – सख़्त – Enforcing rules firmly without allowing flexibility. • Example: The school has strict guidelines regarding attendance. • Flexible – लचीला – Able to change or adapt easily according to situations. • Example: The company offers flexible working hours to employees. • Casual – लापरवाह / अनौपचारिक – Relaxed and unconcerned; not serious or strict. • Example: He has a casual attitude toward deadlines. • Lenient – उदार / नरम – Not strict; allowing more freedom than expected. • Example: The judge was lenient while giving the punishment

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the underlined word in the following sentence The management decided to defer the implementation of the new policy until further consultations with stakeholders were completed.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Advance 💢 Explanation: The word “defer” means to postpone or delay something to a later time. The opposite of postponing is bringing something forward or doing it earlier, which is expressed by the word “advance.” Hence, “advance” is the most appropriate antonym in this context. 👉 Let’s see the meanings of all the words: • Defer – स्थगित करना – To postpone or put off an action to a later time. • Example: The board decided to defer the meeting due to unavoidable circumstances. • Advance – आगे बढ़ाना / शीघ्र करना – To bring forward in time; to do earlier than planned. • Example: The company decided to advance the project deadline. • Postpone – टालना – To delay an event or action to a later time. • Example: They had to postpone the match because of rain. • Delay – विलंब करना – To make something happen later than expected. • Example: Technical issues delayed the flight. • Suspend – अस्थायी रूप से रोकना – To temporarily stop something. • Example: The school suspended classes due to bad weather.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to make the sentence sensibly correct. The report was widely criticised not only ___ its flawed methodology but also ___ its failure to account for regional disparities in data collection.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. for, for 💢 Explanation: The adjective “criticised” is followed by the fixed preposition “for” when the reason or cause of criticism is mentioned. Since both blanks refer to reasons for criticism, the correct construction is “criticised for … for …”.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning for the given idiom. On thin ice
options: Being in a safe and secure position
Facing a risky or dangerous situation
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Facing a risky or dangerous situation 💢 Explanation: The idiom “on thin ice” is used to describe a situation where someone is in danger of getting into trouble or is in a precarious position. Just like thin ice can break at any moment, a person “on thin ice” must act cautiously because one wrong step can lead to serious consequences. 📌 Origin of the Idiom: The idiom comes from the literal danger of walking on thin ice, which may crack or break suddenly. Over time, this physical risk became a metaphor for social, professional, or personal risk, where safety is uncertain. 👉 Example: After repeated warnings from his manager, he knew he was on thin ice at work. Hindi – बार-बार चेतावनी मिलने के बाद, उसे पता था कि वह नौकरी में ख़तरे की स्थिति में है।

questions.question: Identify the segment in the sentence that contains grammatical error.
options: During the exhibition, the curator proudly presented a
beautifully crafted large ancient European rectangular
stone monument recovered from the archaeological site
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. beautifully crafted large ancient European rectangular 📌 CONCEPT – Order of Adjectives • When multiple adjectives are placed before a noun, English follows a strict conventional order, which is tested heavily in SSC CGL. 👉 Standard Order of Adjectives: Opinion → Size → Shape → Age → Colour → Origin → Material → Purpose + Noun • Adjectives must follow this order regardless of how natural the sentence sounds. ✅ Correct Order (as per rule) - ✔ beautifully crafted (opinion) → large (size) → rectangular (shape) → ancient (age) → European (origin) → stone (material) → monument (noun) ✅ Correct Sentence - “During the exhibition, the curator proudly presented a beautifully crafted large rectangular ancient European stone monument recovered from the archaeological site.”

questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the given word. METICULOUS
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Thorough 💢 Explanation: The word “meticulous” refers to someone who pays great attention to detail and works with extreme care and precision. The synonym that most closely matches this meaning is “thorough,” which describes doing something carefully and completely, without overlooking details. 👉 Let’s see the meaning of all the words: • Meticulous – सूक्ष्म / अत्यंत सावधान – Showing great attention to detail; very careful and precise. Example: She is meticulous in maintaining financial records. • Careless – लापरवाह – Not paying proper attention or lacking care. Example: A careless mistake cost him the job. • Thorough – पूरी तरह से / सावधानीपूर्वक – Done carefully and completely, with attention to every detail. Example: The audit was thorough and well documented. • Hasty – जल्दबाज़ी में किया गया – Done too quickly without enough thought. Example: He made a hasty decision under pressure. • Negligent – लापरवाह / उपेक्षापूर्ण – Failing to take proper care, often leading to harm. Example: The doctor was accused of negligent behavior.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the following sentence – Despite repeated provocations and deliberate attempts to derail the discussion, the negotiator remained conciliatory, focusing on compromise rather than confrontation.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Aggressive 💢 Explanation: The word “conciliatory” describes a person who aims to reduce conflict, promote compromise, and maintain harmony. The opposite of such behavior is “aggressive,” which implies hostility, forcefulness, and a tendency to escalate conflict rather than resolve it. 👉 Let’s see the meaning of all the words: • Conciliatory – समझौतापूर्ण / मेल-मिलाप वाला – Intended to calm tensions and promote agreement. Example: He used a conciliatory tone to settle the dispute. • Aggressive – आक्रामक – Forceful, hostile, or ready to attack or argue. Example: His aggressive response worsened the situation. • Diplomatic – कूटनीतिक – Tactful and careful in handling sensitive matters (near synonym). Example: She gave a diplomatic reply to avoid controversy. • Accommodating – सहयोगी / अनुकूल – Willing to adjust or help others (near synonym). Example: The hotel staff was very accommodating. • Amiable – मिलनसार – Friendly and pleasant in nature (emotional synonym trap). Example: He is known for his amiable personality.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blanks. The judge could not ______ the witness’s testimony as reliable, and even a brief ______ during the hearing created confusion in the courtroom.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. accept, pause 👉 This is the correct choice because “accept” means to consider something as valid or true, which fits the first blank, and “pause” refers to a short break or stop, which logically completes the second part of the sentence. 💢 Explanation of all Homophones / Confusable Words: • Accept – स्वीकार करना – To agree to or consider something as valid or true. Example: The committee refused to accept the proposal. • Except – के अलावा – Used to exclude something from a general statement. Example: Everyone except Rohan was present. • Pause – विराम / ठहराव – A short break or stop in action or speech. Example: She took a pause before answering the question. • Paws – पंजे – The feet of animals like cats or dogs. Example: The puppy cleaned its paws.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words. Deliberately kept unclear in order to mislead or confuse
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Equivocal 💢 Explanation of all the words: • Ambiguous – द्विअर्थी – Having more than one possible meaning; unclear but not necessarily intentional. Example: His ambiguous reply left everyone confused. • Vague – अस्पष्ट – Not clearly expressed; lacking detail, often due to carelessness. Example: She gave a vague explanation of the problem. • Obscure – धुंधला / कठिन – Difficult to understand or not well known; may be unclear due to complexity. Example: The law uses obscure language. • Equivocal – जानबूझकर अस्पष्ट – Intentionally unclear in order to deceive or avoid commitment. Example: The politician gave an equivocal answer to avoid controversy. 👉 Therefore, “equivocal” is the correct choice, because it uniquely includes the idea of deliberate ambiguity, which the others do not.

questions.question: Arrange the following sentences (P, Q, R, S) to form a coherent and logically structured paragraph: P. This shift, though gradual, has altered how individuals evaluate credibility, often valuing immediacy and familiarity over verification and depth. Q. In the absence of traditional editorial filters, information now circulates rapidly, shaped largely by user behaviour rather than institutional oversight. R. Over time, digital platforms have emerged not merely as channels of communication but as primary gatekeepers of public attention and opinion. S. Consequently, narratives that align with prevailing sentiments tend to gain traction, while dissenting or complex perspectives struggle for visibility.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. R Q P S 💢 Explanation - • R introduces the broadest conceptual frame: digital platforms as gatekeepers of attention and opinion. It does not rely on any prior reference (this, such, consequently), making it a valid independent opener. • Q explains the mechanism behind R’s claim. “In the absence of traditional editorial filters” logically expands on why platforms function as gatekeepers. • P begins with “This shift”, which clearly refers to the system described in R + Q (platform-driven, behaviour-shaped information flow). P moves the paragraph from system-level change → cognitive impact on individuals. • S starts with “Consequently”, signaling an inevitable outcome. It describes the final social effect: dominance of popular narratives and marginalization of complexity.

questions.question: Select the option that best expresses the given sentence in the passive voice: No one had informed the candidates that the schedule might be revised.
options: The candidates were not informed that the schedule might be revised.
The candidates had not been informed that the schedule might be revised.
It was not informed to the candidates that the schedule might be revised.
That the schedule might revised had not been informed to the candidates.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. The candidates had not been informed that the schedule might be revised. 💢 Explanation: The active sentence is in the past perfect tense (had informed). While converting to passive, the object “the candidates” becomes the subject, and the tense must remain past perfect passive → had not been informed. Option (2) preserves both the tense and the meaning accurately.

questions.question: Select the option that correctly converts the given sentence into Indirect Speech: The officer said to the witness, “Do not conceal any fact, or the truth will never come out.”
options: The officer told the witness that he should not conceal any fact or the truth would never come out.
The officer ordered the witness not to conceal any fact and said that the truth would never come out.
The officer warned the witness not conceal any fact, or the truth would never come out.
The officer warned the witness not to conceal any fact, otherwise the truth would never come out.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. The officer warned the witness not to conceal any fact, otherwise the truth would never come out. 💢 Explanation: The sentence contains an imperative along with a result-based warning. Hence, the reporting verb “warned” is most appropriate. In indirect speech, “will” changes to “would”, and “or” is correctly replaced by “otherwise” to maintain the conditional sense. Option (4) preserves both meaning and structure.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 1 –
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. implemented 💢 Explanation - The sentence contrasts projection with outcome. While governments may announce or promise policies, real economic results depend on whether those policies are actually put into practice at the ground level. Implemented means executed or carried out, which directly links policy formulation to real-world impact. Hence, this word fits both the economic logic and the contextual contrast of the sentence. 👉 Why Other Options Are Wrong - • Option 1 – articulated means clearly expressed or stated. Governments do articulate policies in budget speeches, but articulation alone does not determine outcomes. The sentence specifically focuses on ground-level execution, not verbal clarity, making this option contextually insufficient. • Option 3 – legislated refers to the process of passing laws through a legislature. While some budgetary measures require legislation, economic outcomes depend not merely on legal approval but on administrative execution. Thus, legislated stops at the legal stage and does not guarantee real impact. • Option 4 – publicized means promoted or made widely known. Public awareness of policies does not ensure effectiveness or success. The sentence clearly moves beyond announcement and visibility to practical enforcement, making this option irrelevant in context.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 2 –
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. unchecked 💢 Explanation - The sentence warns about the consequences of excessive borrowing leading to a widening fiscal deficit. If a fiscal deficit is left without control or restraint, it can seriously harm macroeconomic stability. Unchecked means not controlled, monitored, or limited, which perfectly aligns with the economic idea that an unrestrained deficit poses systemic risks. 👉 Why Other Options Are Wrong – • Option 2 – incidental means occurring as a minor or secondary result. A fiscal deficit threatening macroeconomic stability cannot be treated as accidental or insignificant, so this option weakens the seriousness of the context. • Option 3 – provisional means temporary or subject to change. The sentence does not talk about temporariness but about lack of control, making this option contextually incorrect. • Option 4 – marginal refers to something small or negligible. A deficit that undermines macroeconomic stability is by definition not marginal, so this option contradicts the core idea of the sentence.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 3 –
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. calibrated 💢 Explanation - The sentence refers to budgetary allocations for social welfare and stresses the need for careful planning so that resources reach the intended beneficiaries. Calibrated means carefully adjusted or finely tuned, which fits the economic context where allocations must be precisely planned to balance efficiency, targeting, and impact. This word reflects deliberate and measured decision-making in budget formulation. 👉 Why Other Options Are Wrong – • Option 2 – inflated means artificially increased or exaggerated. Inflated allocations suggest excess or distortion, not careful planning, and would increase inefficiency rather than improve targeting. • Option 3 – diverted means redirected or misused. The sentence talks about proper allocation to beneficiaries, not misuse of funds, making this option contextually incorrect. • Option 4 – fragmented refers to something broken into uncoordinated parts. Fragmented allocations would hinder effective delivery of welfare benefits, directly opposing the intended meaning of the sentence.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 4 –
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. however. 💢 Explanation - The sentence contrasts an ideal policy preference with actual practice. While capital expenditure is generally preferred over revenue spending from a policy perspective, short-term political considerations often distort this balance. However is the most appropriate connector because it introduces a clear contradiction between what should ideally happen and what often happens in reality. 👉 Why Other Options Are Wrong – • Option 1 – therefore indicates a result or logical consequence. Here, the second clause does not follow as a result of the first; instead, it opposes it. • Option 3 – similarly is used to show similarity or comparison, but the two clauses are not similar in nature; they are contrasting. • Option 4 – meanwhile relates to time or simultaneity, not to logical contrast, making it unsuitable in this context.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 5 –
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. between 💢 Explanation - The fixed construction balance between X and Y is grammatically and idiomatically correct. Here, it connects growth imperatives and fiscal discipline. 👉 WHY OTHER OPTIONS ARE WRONG: • Among is used for more than two elements. • Against suggests opposition, not balance. • Over is incorrect in this structure.
